# Timeline of 2007

A chronological overview of notable events from the year 2007.

<!-- BEGIN GENERATED: timeline — maintained by build-structure; do not edit by hand -->
| Date | Event | Category |
|---|---|---|
| Jan 9, 2007 | [iPhone launched](science-technology/iphone-launch.md) — Apple CEO Steve Jobs announced the original iPhone at Macworld, describing it as a widescreen iPod, revolutionary phone, and internet communicator in one device. | Science & Technology |
| Jan 10, 2007 | [Iraq War Troop Surge announced](history-politics/iraq-war-troop-surge.md) — President George W. Bush announced a "surge" of roughly 30,000 additional U.S. troops to Iraq, shifting to population-centric counterinsurgency under General David Petraeus. | History & Politics |
| Feb 2, 2007 | [IPCC Fourth Assessment Report released](science-technology/ipcc-fourth-assessment-report.md) — The IPCC began releasing its landmark Fourth Assessment Report (Working Group I, Paris), concluding with at least 90% certainty that human greenhouse-gas emissions caused most observed warming since the mid-20th century. | Science & Technology |
| Feb 8, 2007 | [Anna Nicole Smith dies](people/anna-nicole-smith.md) — The model, actress, and reality-television star died at age 39 in a Hollywood, Florida, hotel room from an accidental combined-drug intoxication; her estate litigation produced two U.S. Supreme Court decisions. | People |
| Feb 16, 2007 | [Britney Spears shaves her head](arts-culture/britney-spears-head-shaving-2007.md) — During an acute personal crisis and a custody dispute, the pop star shaved her head at a Tarzana salon; the heavily photographed event became a defining moment in celebrity tabloid and mental-health discourse. | Arts & Culture |
| Apr 11, 2007 | [Kurt Vonnegut dies](people/kurt-vonnegut.md) — The acclaimed American author of *Slaughterhouse-Five*, humorist, and humanist died in New York City at age 84 from brain injuries sustained in a fall, ending one of the most influential careers in postwar American literature. | People |
| Apr 12, 2007 | [Imus in the Morning cancelled](arts-culture/imus-in-the-morning-cancellation.md) — CBS Radio cancelled Don Imus's long-running program after his April 4 racial remarks about the Rutgers women's basketball team triggered civil-rights protest and mass advertiser withdrawal. | Arts & Culture |
| Apr 16, 2007 | [Virginia Tech shooting](history-politics/virginia-tech-shooting.md) — Seung-Hui Cho killed 32 students and faculty at Virginia Tech in Blacksburg, Virginia, the deadliest mass shooting in modern U.S. history to that point. | History & Politics |
| Apr 23, 2007 | [Boris Yeltsin dies](people/boris-yeltsin.md) — Russia's first post-Soviet president died of congestive heart failure in Moscow aged 76; President Putin declared a national day of mourning on the day of his funeral. | People |
| May 2007 | [YouTube rises as global media platform](science-technology/youtube-growth-2007.md) — Following its 2006 Google acquisition, YouTube surpassed 100 million daily video views and launched its revenue-sharing Partner Program, formalizing the creator economy before co-hosting CNN's first citizen-question presidential debates in July. | Science & Technology |
| May 24, 2007 | [Facebook Platform opens to developers](science-technology/facebook-platform.md) — Facebook unveiled its developer platform at the first F8 conference, enabling third-party applications on the social network and accelerating its growth past MySpace in global traffic. | Science & Technology |
| Jun 3, 2007 | [Paris Hilton jail sentence](people/paris-hilton-jail-2007.md) — The heiress and reality-television personality began a 23-day jail term in Los Angeles County for violating probation from a 2006 drunk-driving case; a brief release to house arrest and court-ordered return generated saturation media coverage. | People |
| Jun 27, 2007 | [Gordon Brown becomes UK Prime Minister](history-politics/gordon-brown-becomes-pm.md) — Gordon Brown succeeded Tony Blair as UK Prime Minister after a decade as Chancellor of the Exchequer, in the first mid-Parliament change of premier since 1990 and without a general election. | History & Politics |
| Jun 29, 2007 | [iPhone released](science-technology/iphone-launch.md) — The first iPhone went on sale in the United States at $499–$599, with thousands of customers lining up at Apple stores nationwide. | Science & Technology |
| Jul 2007 | [Subprime mortgage crisis erupts](society-economics/subprime-mortgage-crisis.md) — Bear Stearns disclosed that two of its subprime hedge funds had lost nearly all their value, signaling the onset of the global financial crisis. | Society & Economics |
| Jul 7, 2007 | [Live Earth 2007 concerts](arts-culture/live-earth-2007.md) — More than 150 acts performed across twelve venues on all seven continents in a global benefit concert for climate-change awareness, produced with Al Gore's Alliance for Climate Protection. | Arts & Culture |
| Jul 21, 2007 | [*Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows* published](arts-culture/harry-potter-deathly-hallows.md) — J. K. Rowling's seventh and final Harry Potter novel sold 8.3 million copies in the first 24 hours in the U.S. alone, setting a Guinness World Record. | Arts & Culture |
| Jul 30, 2007 | [Ingmar Bergman dies](people/ingmar-bergman.md) — The Swedish filmmaker behind *The Seventh Seal* and *Wild Strawberries*, widely regarded as one of cinema's greatest directors, died at age 89 on Fårö the same day as Italian director Michelangelo Antonioni. | People |
| Jul 31, 2007 | [Darfur conflict escalates internationally](history-politics/darfur-conflict-2007.md) — UN Security Council Resolution 1769 authorized the AU–UN hybrid UNAMID force—the largest peacekeeping mission yet approved—amid ICC war-crimes warrants against Sudanese officials and a renewed U.S. genocide designation over the world's worst humanitarian crisis. | History & Politics |
| Aug 1, 2007 | [I-35W Bridge collapses](history-politics/i35w-bridge-collapse.md) — The eight-lane Interstate 35W steel truss bridge over the Mississippi River in Minneapolis collapsed during rush hour, killing 13 and injuring 145; the NTSB later traced the failure to undersized gusset plates. | History & Politics |
| Aug 6, 2007 | [Russia-Georgia conflict escalates](history-politics/russia-georgia-conflict-2007.md) — A Russian military aircraft violated Georgian airspace and fired a missile near Tsitelubani, the most serious of the 2007 incidents over South Ossetia and Abkhazia that foreshadowed the August 2008 Russo-Georgian War. | History & Politics |
| Aug 17, 2007 | [Federal Reserve launches its 2007 crisis response](society-economics/ben-bernanke-fed-2007.md) — Ben Bernanke's Fed cut the discount rate 50 basis points, the first move of a 100-basis-point easing campaign that, with the December Term Auction Facility and international swap lines, sought to thaw frozen credit markets. | Society & Economics |
| Sep 6, 2007 | [Luciano Pavarotti dies](people/luciano-pavarotti.md) — The Italian tenor, widely regarded as the greatest operatic voice of the 20th century, died of pancreatic cancer at his home in Modena, aged 71. | People |
| Sep 16, 2007 | [Arctic sea ice hits record low](science-technology/arctic-sea-ice-record-low.md) — Arctic sea ice extent fell to a satellite-era minimum of 4.13 million km², about 24% below the 2005 record, opening the Northwest Passage to icebreaker-free navigation for the first time in recorded history. | Science & Technology |
| Oct 9, 2007 | [Stock market peaks before 2008 crisis](society-economics/stock-market-peak-october-2007.md) — The Dow Jones Industrial Average closed at an all-time high of 14,164.53, the zenith of the 2002–2007 bull market before the global financial crisis. | Society & Economics |
| Oct 12, 2007 | [Al Gore and IPCC share Nobel Peace Prize](history-politics/al-gore-ipcc-nobel-peace-prize.md) — The Nobel Committee awarded the 2007 Peace Prize jointly to Al Gore and the IPCC for their work building consensus on man-made climate change. | History & Politics |
| Nov 5, 2007 | [Writers Guild of America strike](arts-culture/writers-guild-strike.md) — WGA members began a strike over compensation for internet-distributed content, halting U.S. television production for 100 days and reshaping residual-pay rules. | Arts & Culture |
| Nov 19, 2007 | [Amazon Kindle launched](science-technology/amazon-kindle-launch.md) — Amazon introduced the Kindle e-reader at $399, selling out within 5.5 hours and fundamentally changing how consumers purchase and read books. | Science & Technology |
| Nov 20, 2007 | [Induced pluripotent stem cells created](science-technology/induced-pluripotent-stem-cells.md) — The teams of Shinya Yamanaka (*Cell*) and James Thomson (*Science*) independently reprogrammed adult human cells into induced pluripotent stem cells, opening regenerative medicine without embryo destruction. | Science & Technology |
| Nov 21, 2007 | [*No Country for Old Men* released](arts-culture/no-country-for-old-men.md) — The Coen Brothers' adaptation of Cormac McCarthy's novel went into wide release, winning four Academy Awards including Best Picture at the 80th ceremony in February 2008. | Arts & Culture |
| Nov 21, 2007 | [Oil prices spike to near $100 per barrel](society-economics/oil-price-spike-2007.md) — Crude oil futures reached $99.29 per barrel, the closest prices had ever come to the historic $100 threshold, signaling a structural shift in global energy markets. | Society & Economics |
| Dec 6, 2007 | [Treasury brokers subprime ARM rate-freeze plan](society-economics/hank-paulson-treasury-2007.md) — Hank Paulson's Treasury announced a voluntary five-year freeze on resetting subprime adjustable-rate mortgages, the centerpiece of its 2007 housing-crisis response alongside the Hope Now Alliance. | Society & Economics |
| Dec 27, 2007 | [Benazir Bhutto assassinated](history-politics/benazir-bhutto-assassination.md) — Pakistan's former prime minister was shot and killed in Rawalpindi after a political rally, destabilizing the country ahead of scheduled January elections. | History & Politics |
<!-- END GENERATED: timeline -->
